purpose: Define the single source of truth, read timing, write boundary, and review requirement for <ORG_DIR> agent rules and knowledge.
principles: Read the registry first, then load only task-relevant rules.
Feishu knowledge base is primarily for humans.
Company.md is the company fact baseline.
<GRAPH_DIAGNOSTICS_TOOL> is a diagnostic candidate layer, not an authoritative knowledge source.
Known common errors are preflight inputs before risky actions.

CRM access is scoped by Feishu open_id to CRM backend account mapping; Feishu CRM actions are allowed only in Sales Work Group or private chats where the backend maps the sender to an active CRM account; admins have global visibility only inside allowed CRM entry points, while high-risk writes still require confirmation and audit.
Recordings, audio, video, and minutes never imply CRM matching; even Sales Work Group audio requires a separate explicit text CRM command before any CRM operation.
Engineering changes follow Karpathy-style assumption, simplicity, surgical-change, and verification discipline.
Local Codex project memories accelerate project handoff and continuation, but do not replace project files or <AGENT_KB_NAME> project maps.
Global <MEMORY_TOOL> common-error memories accelerate recall but do not replace COMMON_ERRORS.md or the machine-readable registry.
Group and employee context files are local working memory, not official company fact sources.
Feishu group and employee conversations are isolated by current chat_id/open_id routing; local filesystem readability is not authorization to read another group or employee context.

Company-material tasks must read company_sources.md, Company.md, the <AGENT_KB_NAME> agent knowledge entry, and any necessary Feishu knowledge base current-version reference before answering from company files.
Agent entry files stay slim; detailed reusable rules move to agent_rules or <AGENT_KB_NAME> by confirmation level.
High-risk updates become review candidates before publication.

sources.label: Live root agent rules
sources.read_when: Every <ORG_DIR> runtime task, Feishu workflow, worker execution, sub-agent route, and live agent system maintenance task.

sources.label: Common error preflight
sources.read_when: Before risky tasks involving config or script writes, Chinese or long command parameters, model capacity, Codex remote 403/compact failures, context length, hidden private directories, Git publication, <CONNECTOR_NAME>, hooks, session repair, pre-model connector failures, or any task that should avoid repeated known Codex walls.

sources.label: Machine-readable common error wall avoidance registry
sources.read_when: Before worker execution, hook delivery, connector recovery, Codex remote 403/compact recovery, risky file/message/send operations, and when repeated runtime failures should be turned into do_not_try/use_instead/verify guidance.

sources.label: Global Common Error <MEMORY_TOOL> memories
sources.read_when: When <MEMORY_TOOL> is available and a task touches known high-frequency failure modes, especially connector, hook, worker, automation, memory, delivery, Git publication, encoding, context, model runtime, or workspace search issues.

sources.label: Local Codex project memory
sources.read_when: When the user asks what a project is for, where it left off, what to do next, or asks Codex to continue, organize, resume, review, or maintain a long-running project.

sources.label: Common error update candidates
sources.read_when: During weekly memory compression, governance review, or after a new generic engineering/runtime failure is observed.

sources.label: Agent governance review playbook
sources.read_when: When organizing, reviewing, consolidating, or improving agent rules, context, hooks, workers, automations, sub-agent routing, memory rules, delivery behavior, knowledge drafts, common errors, governance checks, GitHub templates, or after upgrading agent runtime/execution behavior.

sources.label: Agent system slimming policy
sources.read_when: When slimming AGENTS.md, SUB_AGENT.md, agent_rules, Company.md, <AGENT_KB_NAME> knowledge pages, or deciding whether a temporary rule should become a formal rule.

sources.label: Company fact baseline
sources.owner: AI admin and confirmed business owners
sources.read_when: Company profile, product facts, business boundary, public-facing wording, or conflict resolution.

sources.label: Company source routing policy
sources.read_when: Before any task involving company materials, company files, product facts, sales or marketing materials, SOPs, FAQs, templates, employee-readable official documents, controlled-material boundaries, or answers that should reference company files rather than model memory.

sources.label: CRM security and account mapping policy
sources.read_when: Before any CRM customer query, lead/customer intake, customer update, ownership assignment, duplicate check, export, CRM analysis, CRM worker task, CRM permission/debugging request, deciding whether a Feishu conversation is an allowed CRM entry point, deciding whether the sender should be validated by CRM backend open_id mapping, or deciding whether a recording/audio/minutes task may trigger CRM matching.
do_not_store: unredacted account mappings in public files

sources.label: Feishu knowledge base
sources.read_when: Employees need readable SOP, FAQ, templates, official material, reusable documentation, training, policy, or current official wording; also when <AGENT_KB_NAME>'s Feishu knowledge-base comparison says the current online version must be verified.

sources.label: <AGENT_KB_NAME> agent knowledge vault
sources.read_when: Agent needs company-file retrieval maps, topic maps, task manuals, execution paths, permission checks, knowledge draft routing, or a company-material answer that should not rely only on model memory.

sources.label: <GRAPH_DIAGNOSTICS_TOOL> relationship diagnostics
sources.path: graphify-out + <RULES_DIR>/graph_diagnostics.md + <ORG_DIR>/知识库/<COMPANY_SHORT_NAME>/99_<AGENT_NAME>工作台/<GRAPH_DIAGNOSTICS_TOOL>关系诊断候选.md
sources.read_when: When using <GRAPH_DIAGNOSTICS_TOOL> outputs to inspect rule relationships, code/document links, isolated knowledge nodes, conflict candidates, template/live-rule confusion, or knowledge graph health.

sources.label: Company agent rules
sources.read_when: Task matches an indexed rule scenario.

sources.label: Daily review core
sources.read_when: Daily review, department memory update, or knowledge draft routing.

sources.label: Hook guardrail policy
sources.read_when: Changing hooks, CRM intake gates, outbound delivery, artifact delivery, memory writes, or automation writeback.

sources.label: Xiaogongdan OpenAPI safety policy
sources.read_when: When reading Xiaogongdan data, generating reports, preparing Feishu image/OCR import drafts, or performing any Xiaogongdan create, update, status, output, stock, process, or process routing operation.
do_not_store: raw external-system payloads with private business data

sources.label: XiaoQuan background worker runtime policy
sources.read_when: Creating, routing, executing, recovering, debugging, or migrating XiaoQuan background worker tasks, long-running automations, or codex exec jobs.

sources.label: Context budget policy
sources.read_when: Updating group_context.md, long_term_context.md, SUB_AGENT.md, tasks/TODO.md, or weekly compression rules.

sources.label: Group agent entry
sources.read_when: Handling a message routed to that group.

sources.label: Group machine-readable profile
sources.read_when: Automation or hook needs to know allowed sends, writes, redaction, or cross-group behavior.
do_not_store: chat_id beyond declared group id if not necessary

sources.label: Group long-term context
sources.owner: Group agent under AI admin rules
sources.read_when: Handling a message routed to that group or running approved group review.

sources.label: Group external memory pilot rules
sources.read_when: When a group enables an external memory layer such as <MEMORY_TOOL>, when recalling group-specific long-term preferences from an external memory tool, or when deciding whether a memory may be written outside local files.

sources.label: Employee long-term context
sources.owner: Employee context automation under AI admin rules
sources.read_when: Handling an identified employee private chat.

sources.label: Knowledge intake status table
sources.read_when: Creating, reviewing, publishing, indexing, rejecting, or archiving knowledge candidates.

sources.label: Codex automations
sources.read_when: Creating or updating scheduled reviews, monitors, delivery jobs, or validation jobs.
